what_works: robots.txt returns 200, allows general crawling, and references the XML sitemap
No explicit GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, CCBot, or Applebot-Extended blocks were found
Homepage has clear title, H1, Organization/EducationalOrganization schema, branch schema, and phone CTA
Sitemap access is healthy for crawler discovery

findings.title: llms.txt is missing
findings.description: https://zehnati.ir/llms.txt returns 404, so LLM crawlers do not receive a curated summary of the brand, expert, services, locations, phone, and preferred citation pages.
findings.recommendation: Publish /llms.txt with canonical facts, service summaries, official URLs, branch information, and preferred source pages.

findings.title: AI citation pages are not strong enough
findings.description: The site lacks stable, easy-to-quote pages for online consultation, pricing, packages, city branches, expert biography, methodology, registration, planning, selection, and outcomes.
findings.recommendation: Create concise factual pages and sections that AI systems can quote directly, supported by schema and internal links.

findings.title: Broken P0 URLs reduce source reliability
findings.description: Important source URLs such as /مشاوره-آنلاین-کنکور/, /اصفهان/, and /تهران/ return 404, while several commercial short slugs redirect to mismatched articles.
findings.recommendation: Publish or repair the P0 pages so AI systems can resolve service and location claims to stable canonical URLs.

findings.title: Entity graph is thin beyond the website itself
findings.description: Structured sameAs/citation signals are mostly self-referential, and verified external profiles such as GBP or social profiles are not strongly connected in schema.
findings.recommendation: Add verified sameAs links for official social profiles, GBP where eligible, Aparat/Rubika/Soroush/Bale profiles as appropriate, and advisor credentials.

findings.title: Stale fish-programmer signal remains
findings.description: The homepage rendered HTML still contains fish-programmer, which can confuse author/entity interpretation for an expert-led education service.
findings.recommendation: Remove or replace stale fish-programmer author/entity metadata with Dr. Zahra Jafari or a verified Mind Ati organization identity.
